commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=748a5699e6fb39b9ab5d035974bde774a959dc36)
commands.review_summary:
The PR replaces Kiro's `Process`-based runner with a package-internal `posix_spawn` process-group launcher, residual-helper cleanup, EOF-aware pipe capture, and focused process tests.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. A fake Kiro parent can exit normally after forking a same-process-group helper that closes stdout/stderr and ignores `TERM`; the PR then reaches residual cleanup with `isRunning == false` and no pipe holder to signal.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Owner-authored low-level process cleanup needs maintainer review after fixing the normal-exit same-group helper leak; preserving PID-reuse safety is not a trivial automated patch.

target.title: fix: clean up Kiro CLI helper processes
target.body:
## Summary

- launch Kiro CLI commands with a dedicated process group established before `exec`
- terminate residual helpers after normal parent exit, escalating from `TERM` to `KILL` before draining inherited pipes
- discover escaped helpers by the exact stdout/stderr pipes they retain
- snapshot descendants and same-group members while the root process identity is still valid
- signal only processes whose start-time identity still matches, avoiding PID or process-group reuse hazards
- preserve child standard streams when the host starts with a standard descriptor closed
- preserve existing timeout, cancellation, output, and exit-status behavior

Follow-up to https://github.com/steipete/CodexBar/issues/1533 and https://github.com/steipete/CodexBar/issues/1535.

## Root Cause

The original Kiro runner could wait indefinitely after termination or parent exit: `waitUntilExit()` had no `KILL` escalation, and final EOF reads remained blocked while descendants retained inherited output pipes.

The first bounded repair established a process group before `exec`, but helpers could still escape it with `setsid`, or become reparented while remaining in the original group. The final candidate records stable identities for output pipes, descendants, the root, and current process-group members while the root identity is known to be current. All later signals are sent only to those start-time-verified identities. Normal-exit cleanup remains limited to exact output-pipe holders, and no numeric process-group signal is sent after root exit.

## Proof

Exact head: `748a5699e6fb39b9ab5d035974bde774a959dc36`

- focused Kiro/process tests: 38 passed, including inherited-pipe, escaped-session, reparented same-group, grace-period, ignored-`TERM`, cancellation, and closed-standard-descriptor cases
- `make test`: 470 deterministic selections across 40 shards passed; no Swift Testing issue markers
- `make check`: parser/package/release guards passed; SwiftFormat clean; SwiftLint 0 violations across 1117 files
- `git diff --check`: passed
- `swift build -c release --product CodexBarCLI --skip-update`: passed
- release CLI normal-exit proof: parent exited normally while a session-escaped helper retained inherited output pipes and ignored `HUP`/`TERM`; the command returned parsed usage in 2.675s and removed the helper
- release CLI idle-termination proof: a reparented same-group helper held no output pipes and ignored `TERM`; the command returned parsed usage in 10.984s and removed the helper
- both live proofs returned exit 0, emitted no stderr, and produced parsed Kiro usage without an error
- release CLI: arm64 Mach-O, ad-hoc signed, SHA-256 `b06ae58f7a0e07324f3ac7694dc14352f0ca0b8a713c4558320cb9680ea3b185`
- exact-diff local autoreview: no accepted or actionable findings
- Public Model Identifier Gate: PASS; the exact diff, added lines, build log, and public proof add no model identifiers, while broad matches in full logs and the binary are unchanged repository/provider surface

The runtime proof used an isolated temporary home and synthetic account data. It did not access live credentials, browser data, Keychain, private payloads, or user-identifying paths.

## Risk

The new process launcher is package-internal and currently used only by Kiro. Pipe-holder discovery runs only for residual groups or failed EOF drain. Darwin uses `proc_listpids` for process-group enumeration; Linux filters `/proc`. Every terminating signal is gated by a stable process start identity, and normal successful exit cannot target unrelated members of a reused numeric process group.
